name: Pineapple-Pecan Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Use a 14-ounce can of crushed pineapple. Invert it over a sieve placed in a bowl to drain the pineapple thoroughly.
2. Chop the pecans coarsely.
3. Combine the drained pineapple, sugar, and heavy cream in a heavy saucepan. Stir over low heat until the sugar is dissolved. Increase the heat and bring the mixture to boiling. Cook, stirring occasionally to prevent scorching. Continue cooking until the mixture reaches 234°F on a candy thermometer (soft ball stage). Do not scrape the bottom and sides of the pan; brushing down the side occasionally will help.
4. Remove from heat and let cool just until you can touch the pan without burning yourself. Do not stir or disturb the mixture while it is cooling.
5. Add the margarine (or butter) and vanilla extract. Beat vigorously until the candy loses its gloss. Stir in the pecans using as little mixing as possible.
6. Immediately pour into an 8 x 8 inch greased pan. Do not scrape the bottom or sides of the pan when placing the mixture in the dish.
7. Let cool completely, then cut into small pieces for serving.
